November 17,2009
Marjorie Swartz
Principal Consultant
Assembly Committee on Health
State Capitol, Room 6005
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-220
Dear Ms. Swartz:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act. I Per our telephone conversation of November 16, 2009,
you stated you no longer wish to receive written advice. Therefore, yaur request for advice has
been withdrawn.
Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
ERjgl
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Marjorie Swartz requested advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but subsequently withdrew the request.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges the withdrawal of the advice request as communicated during a November 16, 2009 telephone conversation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter, not an advice letter with substantive Q&A sections. The document confirms that the requester no longer wished to receive written advice. File number W-09-220 indicates this was a formal advice request that was withdrawn.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Marjorie Swartz requested advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but subsequently withdrew the request.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges the withdrawal of the advice request as communicated during a November 16, 2009 telephone conversation.

embedding.summary: On November 17, 2009, the FPPC General Counsel's office sent a letter to Marjorie Swartz confirming the withdrawal of her request for written advice on conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, which she had requested to withdraw via telephone.
